Mtn To Strike Costs And What Justice Is And The Difference Between Rationalization And Justice
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Ryan Davis (415-689-0077; ryan@zendevelop.com), Plaintiff in propria persona.
Bernice K. Wu (415-397-2700; BKW@dillinghammurphy.com) of Dillingham & Murphy, LLP for Defendant Cognii, Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RYAN DAVIS' Motion To Strike Costs And What Justice Is And The Difference Between Rationalization And Justice:

Plaintiff's motion to strike costs is denied. Defendant is the prevailing party and entitled to recover its costs "as a matter of right." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1032(b).) The claimed costs are recoverable pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 1033.5(a)(1) and (11) ["The following items are allowable as costs under Section 1032: (1) Filing, motion, and jury fees... (11) Court reporter fees as established by statute."].) Plaintiff fails to meet his burden of demonstrating that the claimed costs are not allowed or unreasonable. (See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 131 [if items appear to be proper charges, burden is on objecting party to show that they are not properly chargeable or unreasonable].)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
